doc.title: Sell a debt investment before maturity
doc.event: Two years after buying {face} of {statedRateAnnual} bonds at a market rate of {marketRateAnnual}, and just after the fourth coupon, Juno Co. sells the bonds. Prepare the sale entry.

variants.label: Sold at 480,000 — above the period-4 carrying amount

entries.caption: Sell after period 4 for 480,000 (carrying amount = schedule row 4)

lines.label: sale proceeds
lines.trap: Bring amortization current to the sale date FIRST — the carrying amount you compare against must be up to date.

lines.label: amortized carrying amount off
lines.why: The investment leaves at its schedule value — cost plus every amortization step taken so far.
lines.trap: Not at original cost (461,391) and not at face — the amortized number in between.

lines.label: proceeds − carrying amount
lines.why: Realized in income — an actual transaction, unlike the unrealized holding adjustments elsewhere in this chapter.
lines.trap: Comparing proceeds to ORIGINAL COST double-counts the amortization you already recognized as revenue.

lines.label: sale proceeds
lines.trap: Same discipline: amortize to the sale date first.

lines.label: carrying amount − proceeds
lines.why: The plug lands on the debit side this time.
lines.trap: The gain/loss is a PLUG after cash and the carrying amount are placed — wrong side means re-check the carrying amount.

lines.label: carrying amount off
lines.trap: The investment account must land on exactly zero for these bonds — a remainder means stale amortization.

memorize.body: Realized gain/loss = proceeds − amortized carrying amount at the sale date (schedule lookup). Identical logic to Ch. 13's early retirement — investor's chair.
Sale between coupon dates: collect and separate the ACCRUED INTEREST (that's interest revenue, not part of the gain/loss math).

questions.prompt: HTM bonds with a period-4 carrying amount are sold for 480,000. The realized gain =

distractors.feedback: That compares to ORIGINAL cost — but four periods of amortization were already recognized as revenue. Compare with the current amortized carrying amount (schedule row 4).
Face is where the schedule ENDS, not where it is today. The comparison base is the amortized carrying amount.

traces.label: Schedule carrying amount ↔ the realized plug

accountBank.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Income

build.scaffold: Three moves: amortize to today, clear the investment at its schedule value, plug the realized gain or loss against the proceeds. 'Unrealized' anything is bait — this sale is real.
